name: Reza
background: Faculty - Data Science and Game Analytics
personality: Enthusiastic and approachable; uses game industry examples extensively; encourages questions; paces quickly through technical material but circles back when students ask; emphasizes domain framing in gaming contexts; assigns exercises that tie DS concepts to game dev scenarios
data_science_experience: Expert in data science with focus on computational experiences and game analytics. Published researcher in the area.
notes: Teaches CMPM 230: Data Science in Computational Experiences. Lectures blend theory with game examples (e.g. analyzing League of Legends match data, Steam review sentiment). Known for clear high-level explanations but sometimes moves quickly through formulas and technical details. Exercises are practical and game-related. Gives students freedom in choosing games/datasets for exercises.

name: Alex Chen
personality: Thoughtful, curious, methodical, asks follow-up questions
data_science_experience: Has done Python data analysis for personal projects but no formal DS coursework
notes: Strong programmer. Appreciates structured approaches. Chose Valorant matchmaking for domain framing exercise.

name: Maya Rodriguez
personality: Visual learner, friendly, sometimes hesitant to speak up about confusion
data_science_experience: Minimal — used spreadsheets for game balance testing
notes: Struggles with technical jargon but has good intuition for design-oriented data questions. Skips Week 2 due to midterm crunch.

name: Jordan Kim
personality: Analytical, confident, connects new concepts to prior coursework
data_science_experience: Completed intro stats course, knows basic R
notes: Often references stats background. Finds some intro material redundant but appreciates game context. Drops off by Week 3 due to project deadlines.

name: Sam Patel
background: Computer Science with Data Science minor
personality: Knowledgeable, constructive, sometimes impatient with basics
data_science_experience: Strong — completed ML course and worked as data analyst intern
notes: Gives detailed constructive feedback. Sometimes wishes the course moved faster. Consistent survey responder.

name: Taylor Williams
personality: Earnest, struggles openly, appreciates encouragement
data_science_experience: None
notes: Finds technical concepts challenging but genuinely tries. Only responds Week 1; gets overwhelmed and stops doing optional surveys.

name: Riley Nakamura
personality: Engaged, asks about ML connections, thorough
data_science_experience: Completed ML intro course, comfortable with pandas/numpy
notes: Very interested in how DS connects to ML pipelines. Provides thoughtful feedback every week. Appreciates rigor.

name: Casey O'Brien
personality: Anxious about coding, creative thinker, relieved when things are visual
data_science_experience: Very minimal — never coded beyond basic HTML
notes: Finds technical material intimidating but appreciates game-related framing. Responds to surveys as a way to process confusion.

name: Morgan Lee
personality: Competent but disengaged from theory, wants hands-on work
data_science_experience: Moderate — has scraped data for side projects
notes: Prefers coding to lectures. Gives brief feedback. Stops responding Week 3.

name: Avery Martinez
personality: Unique perspective, connects to human behavior research, thoughtful
data_science_experience: Has done survey data analysis in R for research
notes: Brings interdisciplinary perspective. Misses Week 2 due to conference travel. Returns Week 3.

name: Jamie Thompson
personality: Practical, industry-focused, wants real-world applicability
data_science_experience: Some — used analytics dashboards at game studio internship
notes: Has industry experience from internship at mobile game studio. Always asks how concepts apply to real production data.

name: Drew Washington
personality: Quiet, submits work but minimal engagement, private
data_science_experience: Basic — followed some online tutorials
notes: Never responds to optional surveys. Attends class and does assignments but avoids extra interaction.

name: Quinn Fischer
personality: Enthusiastic but scattered, gives feedback in bursts
data_science_experience: Minimal — mostly conceptual understanding
notes: Gives energetic but sometimes unfocused feedback. Tends to jump between topics. Stops responding Week 3.

name: Skyler Ahmed
personality: Catching up on tools, appreciates clear step-by-step explanations
data_science_experience: Some Python experience but new to data science libraries
notes: Transfer student still adjusting to UCSC pace. Responds Week 1 only — finds the survey interface confusing and stops.

name: River Santos
background: Game Design and Computer Science (double major)
personality: Balanced perspective, solid understanding, helpful to peers
data_science_experience: Moderate — has done data viz projects and basic analysis
notes: Strong all-around student. Gives balanced and detailed feedback. Consistent responder.

name: Cameron Novak
personality: Perfectionist, asks about edge cases, very detailed
data_science_experience: Strong — research assistant involving data pipelines

name: Jesse Park
personality: Creative, honest about disliking stats, gives blunt feedback
data_science_experience: None
notes: Dislikes quantitative work but enrolled because it was required. Gives honest feedback about not connecting with material. Only responds once.

name: Blake Harrison
personality: Finds early weeks easy, insightful but sometimes comes across as dismissive
data_science_experience: Strong — ML research background
notes: Finds intro material too basic but gives useful perspective on what could challenge others. Stops responding by Week 3.

name: Sage Dimitriou
personality: Interested in UX and player experience data, interdisciplinary
data_science_experience: Survey and behavioral data analysis in R
notes: Only responds Week 2 when data collection connects to their research interests.

name: Hayden Cooper
personality: Works part-time at game studio, time-constrained, sporadic
data_science_experience: Some — uses analytics tools at work but not DS methodology

name: Emery Liu
personality: Strong technical skills, sometimes struggles to articulate feedback in English, polite
data_science_experience: Solid Python and data analysis background from undergrad in China
